title: Link-format parser silently accepts malformed input with unclosed quotes
description:
File: python/src/lichen/client/lci.py, lines 697-719

The _split_quoted function parses link-format entries but doesn't validate that quotes are properly closed:

```python
def _split_quoted(value: str, separator: str) -> list[str]:
    parts: list[str] = []
    current: list[str] = []
    in_quote = False
    escaped = False
    for char in value:
        ...
        if char == '"':
            in_quote = not in_quote
        ...
    parts.append(''.join(current))  # No check that in_quote is False
    return parts
```

Impact: Malformed link-format with unclosed quotes is silently accepted and parsed incorrectly. For example:
- Input: '</foo>;rt="bar' (missing closing quote)
- The parser accepts this and the 'bar' value becomes part of an incorrectly parsed token

This violates RFC 6690 which requires proper quoting.

Fix: After the loop, check if in_quote is True and raise LciClientError with details about the malformed input.
close_reason: Implemented quote validation in _split_quoted per RFC 6690; added regression test; lint/typecheck/tests pass
